review_title: Great database maintenance software - simple and easy to use
description: I've used tools like DemandTools and other database management software in the past - it's all crap compared to RingLead. It's an all-in-one tool that allows me, as a marketing ops person, to link to Marketo and run all my leads through and get them cleaned up (de-duped, matched, converted, etc) as they go through my web forms and uploads. Great tool to also upload big lists into Salesforce. Great tool and I haven't even started to use the more advanced stuff like segments, etc.
author: Cameron E.
pros: Love the simple interface, easy to run and maintain programs, schedules and other filters. Great integrations with Salesforce and Marketo - which is why we
cons: Sometimes have to do some workarounds where they don't have the exact configutation, but for the most part can do everything you need it to.

review_title: Good if you can afford it, otherwise there are hundreds of other cheaper options
description: Good to use if it's already part of your tech stack, but not necessarily worth it if you're a smaller business looking to grow lean. I think of it like an SEMRush which is very expensive, but people love using it if their company can afford it.
author: Diana B.
pros: ZoomInfo is often considered the best prospecting tool if your team can afford it. I love how clean the UI is, and how much data the dashboard shows.
cons: I don't like how you need to contact them to get pricing, that already sets the tone that this isn't a good choice for small to SME's. I think it's quite overpriced compared to the likes of Apollo, Cognism, Hunter, and etc. In my tests, their emails come back as only 85% deliverable on average - and I would say 25% of the 85% are catch-alls. This means they have a closer to 60% accuracy for the types of emails I'd like to find at a very high premium.

description: Deduping
author: Tate S.
pros: The software does indeed work as advertised. They are able to offer deduping services that our company needed.
cons: Wow! Bad experience all around. For 7 months of our 12 month contract they were charging some random persons credit card instead of ours. Apparently they have no safeguard for protecting clients credit card info. Anyway we tried to get it fixed back in November and they just ignored us. We then got an email that they would be contacting their attorney over this. Straight up told me he didn't care if we renewed with them next year. We finally got our credit card on file and hopefully, it's fixed. But there is some serious miscommunication and confusion on their end. Their frustration should never be taken out on a client when it's their own fault. I got ahold of someone else in the company and they apologized profusely for the way we had been treated and for the mistake that had been made on their end and offered us a 10% discount. The software fits our needs for now but we are seriously unimpressed with the customer service and lack of professionalism.

description: Overall, Capture has allowed us to get in contact with candidates who normally we wouldn't have been able to reach. This has greatly increased our ability to be consultative with our clients and fill roles.
author: Danielle T.
pros: I like the fact that we can gather somewhat accurate information on a candidate's personal contact. Being able to keep that information in project lists is helpful as well.
cons: There are only a few things I do not like about the software. In the process of parsing in candidates, there can be some bugs on LinkedIn's side. After that, enriching a large number of candidates can take a while, and sometimes the contact information is incorrect, which has lead to some awkward conversations.

review_title: Easy to understand, difficult to master.
description: Easy to set up, responsive customer service, quality product but difficult to apply well at first.
author: James M.
pros: The fuzzy matching for deduplication of uploads and existing records is fantastic. Even the basic normalization enrichment package will help make your data much more useful. Also, mass update is a fantastic tool for managing your records. This tool has saved me countless hours of coming through duplicates. We would have just had to let them into the system or not uploaded records. It's been a huge win for me in my role as admin.
cons: The deduplication is the part that's difficult to master. It's about as well done as any tool I've seen or tried but the logic for deduplicating can be quite tricky and you have to understand going in that no system is going to completely replace a person reviewing the records.

review_title: Best Bulk Lead Upload Software out there
description: Overall we are happy with it and although every year we review the market we couldn't find any software that would be better than RingLead.
author: Radu S.
pros: We are only using the Lead Upload functionality and for the past 3 years, we had no problem with this. Love the ability to custom match the imported data with the already existing data and the ability to create templates that can be used based on specific matching criteria.
cons: Nothing to mention here, we didn't have any problems with it.

description: I am satified with our overall experience with Ringlead. As they have gone through growth stages and transitions, they maintained a solutions oriented approach. Our impression is that their support has improved as the platform functionalities grew, which we find is not typical in this arena.
author: Philip M.
pros: The Account and Contact merge functionality is something we find very helpful. It is much more effective than native merge functionality.
cons: Initially , we had issues with the deduplication functionality interfering with Contact synchronization on mobile

description: I have been using Ringlead since 6 months for multiples subjects. So far 0 bugs or real issues. And the CS team is always there when need with answers.
author: Paul E.
pros: Every features i use are available on Salesforce, with a big difference. They are way simpler and faster to use on a daily basis. Especially "Deduplication", "mass delete/update". You can also work on both Sandbox and Production effortlessly and refresh the connection immediately.
cons: No big topic. The before last phase seems a bit useless during deduplicating. But that aside nothing.

description: The employees are amazing and very responsive to any need or question I may have. The tool just works!
author: Caterina T.
pros: Easy to set up, easy to test, easy to filter for duplicates, easy to use overall.
cons: Nothing! No complaints here. Team was super helpful and product is easy to use

review_title: How old are these email addresses?
description: A great adjunct to other databases. The occasional personal mobile number makes it worth using. But other products are more current
author: David M.
pros: Availability of mobile phone numbers. This is by far the best reason to use the product. So much better than going the usual route.
cons: Outdated emails. Far too many are from college days. Would like more current data or weed out more of the old ones.

description: RingLead is the platform we use to import all tradeshow / live event lists to our campaigns, and it is used to schedule duplicate resolutions across leads, accounts, and contacts. The platform has come a long way since pre-DMS.
author: tim S.
pros: RingLead has made huge strides with the DMS platform and the tool is flexible enough to run matches on accounts, leads, contacts and leads:accounts and leads:contacts. The tool is easy to use for admins. Match criteria is expansive and flexible, and the Master + Surviving Field Rules are easy to define (and save as templates) to ensure that field and record retention is consistent.
cons: RingLead is a great tool, but there is one thing that bothers me: Intuitively, the way the matching rules are displayed as a cascade makes it seem as if it works like an "if" statement - where the first rules are evaluated first, and if false, then the next set of rules are evaluated. This is not the case; all of the rules are evaluated with the same level of priority. Right now - we would need to create separate de-duplication jobs to run strict, then moderate, then loose matching parameters. It would be HUGE to allow users to prioritize the match rules (i.e. give us an option to prioritize matching logic, ELSE everything will be evaluated at the same priority). Reporting is OK but could definitely be expanded to provide more detail.

description: We onboarded RingLead as a tool to help with the business cards that we got from conferences, but now it is a huge part of our Lead routing and management process. I've also been very very happy with the support we've gotten. Our rep, [SENSITIVE CONTENT HIDDEN], always responds to us right away and sets up calls with more technical people if we need it. My team is able to resolve questions in less than a day - that is so critical to tight project deadlines we typically have. I'm the manager of all of the systems/platforms we have, and RingLead's support is some of the best we get and I have high standards! :) The support we get is definitely a key factor in why we would want to keep RingLead over another platform that does the same thing.
author: Jackie T.
pros: One thing in particular, for the webform submissions, it is awesome to see what errors come up and all the detail that is provided. It makes me really confident in using Ringlead as a tool since we can easily trouble shoot and have a ton of visibility into the data flowing/not flowing through our forms. The interface makes it really easy for people who are not technical to set it up. DMS is so much better than the other interface you had - change is hard to manage, but the newer platform is great!
cons: I'm not a direct end user, but from what I have seen & heard from my team, the interface is really intuitive, so there isn't much to complain about.

review_title: If you're in sales and hate data entry get Capture right away!
description: Capture makes entering new accounts, clients, and leads very quick and painless. The SalesForce process of setting up a new account and then adding a new contact used to take me 15-20 minutes. Now I can do it in 3-5 minutes... with no data errors! For a salesperson, this is a must-have program.
author: Jim W.
pros: When our company started using SalesForce I quickly realized that to get the most out of it I would need to up my data entry game. I tried every program I could find that would automate getting new contacts and leads into SalesForce. Capture was the best back then and has remained the best since.
cons: The only real con is the pricing. When they changed their pricing (a HUGE increase) I immediately went out to find a cheaper alternative. I spent about a week checking out the competition. In the end, I came back to Capture because there is no substitute.

review_title: When it comes to data, ZoomInfo is your to-go!
author: Nacky J.
pros: The UI is intuitive and user-friendly. Clear mapping and quick link to environment. Data count of accounts and contacts are mostly within my satisfactory outcome. Filtering is quick and precise.
cons: Expensive and ZoomInfo will still charge you credits if you download a list that has already been previously downloaded.

review_title: Zoom purchased RingLead and is not supporting Capture enrichment any longer
description: I liked the old capture, I'm still using the "new capture" but I really miss the enrichment. Not to mention the price has gone sky high
author: Valerie S.
pros: It does a great job of capturing linkedin information and putting it in SalesForce
cons: without the enrichment portion to find email information, it's become nothing but a cut and past program

review_title: A great data quality solution for the data focused company
description: From the sales team to customer success and support everyone has been a pleasure to work with. Great integrations with other data enrichment providers and a seamless with SaleForce.
author: Justin C.
pros: I can manage every part of my marketing and sales data process in one platform now.
cons: The design, UX/UI could definitely use an updated. Although the deign looks up to date, the overall experience could be enhanced. A UX update would make this product amazing. Not a showstopper by any means though.

description: We had some issues in implementation that dragged on for months. Customer service was very responsive and helpful, but the product just wasn't a complete solution for both our CRM and email automation platform.
author: Nicol M.
pros: RingLead does a decent job at restricting/preventing duplicates from being created in Salesforce.
cons: It is not a viable solution for preventing/deleting duplicates in Marketo.

description: Its been a very good fit for our lead business.
author: Onttu L.
pros: Allows us to keep our leads clean and prospects flowing.
cons: Refreshing the connection often seems harder then it should be

author: Jessica D.
pros: The ease of use. 100% this program is easy to use, it is straightforward and very helpful.
cons: There are always improvements that can be made. I can't name any at the moment, but improvements are always possible.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: The ability to find prospect's phone numbers. I would give capture 3.5 out of 5 stars
author: Katie M.
pros: Tool is helpful in finding cell phone numbers that otherwise would be unattainable. Very Convenient to be able to upload contacts directly to salesforce
cons: The quality of the phone numbers is lacking. There are times when I am unknowingly calling prospect's spouses or children.

author: Sarah O.
pros: I like that it is easy to configure and it can run automatically on the background without us thinking about it after setting it up.
cons: The UI can be a bit updated. It kinda looks like an older software compared to other software I've used but other than that, the UI is pretty intuitive and nothing is missing.

review_title: Easy Integration with Salesforce and LinkedIn Sales Nav
description: I am quite pleased with this sales tool, and would recommend for any Sales or Business Development team.
author: Angela O.
pros: The most important element when choosing Capture was the integration functionality with SF and LinkedIn Sales Navigator. Capture saves me loads of time because it can easily grab leads from LinkedIn, "capture" lead data, and then easily create the lead in Salesforce.
cons: The one thing I would like to see improvements on with Capture is a higher percentage of emails address accuracy.

description: Overall I like it, and would recommend if you're looking for contact info.
author: Meghan L.
pros: It gets numbers that other companies and services like this can't. It also makes it really easy to get prospects into Salesforce
cons: It often (maybe twice a month or so) stops working for us, which is inconvenient. Sometimes I'll "premium enrich" a contact and nothing will be found and it still uses up one of my credits. Sometimes It'll pull out wrong numbers so you have to be prepared for that when using those numbers to call.

description: Overall experience is good. There are some bugs but it allows me to prospect much easier.
author: Chris B.
pros: I love the ease of use to find contact data on my target prospects then how I’m quickly able to get them into Salesforce
cons: The implementation process was a bit complex getting it fully integrated the way we wanted to with Salesforce

review_title: Strong Platform with Lots of Improvements Recently
description: We use RL to prevent dups from entering our CRM, standardize data, and manage imports/clean up efforts.
author: Andrea S.
pros: Integration w/ SFDC and Marketo and the ability to complete multiple data hygiene tasks from one spot.
cons: I wish it had the ability to mass update records from within RingLead
